title: Mamdani Emerges as Kingmaker, Pushing His Slate to a Primary Sweep
summary: Mayor Zohran Mamdani shook the Democratic establishment by helping drive three progressive candidates to victory.

title: Schlossberg’s Defeat Dampens Dream of a Renewed Camelot
summary: Jack Schlossberg fell well short of his bid to win a highly contested Democratic primary in Manhattan, his campaign hurt by staff defections and missed meetings.

title: Fired Navy Admiral Wins Democratic Runoff in South Carolina’s 1st District
summary: Democrats expect Nancy Lacore to run a competitive general election despite the district’s Republican leaning.

title: Congress Clears Housing Bill, Cementing a Rare Bipartisan Feat
summary: A lopsided House vote cleared the measure for President Trump’s signature after a lengthy back and forth and several nearly fatal blows to the legislation.

title: Trump’s Pick for Top I.R.S. Lawyer Works at Firm That Represents Him
summary: James R. Gadwood, the president’s nominee for chief counsel at the Internal Revenue Service, works at Miller & Chevalier, which has represented Mr. Trump in tax matters.

title: Trump Says Vandals Sabotaged the Reflecting Pool. Internal Documents Raise Doubts.
summary: The documents do not indicate that the peeling blue coating and algae blooms were caused intentionally.

title: Alibaba Sues Pentagon Over China Military Label
summary: The Chinese tech giant said it had no ties to China’s military and that the U.S. government had violated the law by making that claim.

title: N.S.A. Lost Access to Powerful A.I. Model Amid Anthropic Dispute
summary: A recent episode underscored the Trump administration’s increasing reliance on advanced A.I. systems for cybersecurity even as it battles a leading U.S. developer.

title: Trump Is Making Big Claims About the Iran Talks. Iran Keeps Contradicting Him.
summary: President Trump appears to be describing his preferences as fully negotiated deals, in hopes of locking the Iranians in. The question is whether a succession of such disputes will sink the whole venture.

title: Senate Votes to Direct End to Iran War, Rebuking Trump on War Powers
summary: A few Republicans joined Democrats in favor of a measure instructing President Trump to halt military operations against Iran or seek congressional authorization.

title: Myanmar’s Civil War Has Become an Apocalypse
summary: Overshadowed by conflicts in Ukraine, Gaza and Lebanon, the fighting in Myanmar’s isolated heartland has reached new depths.

title: What to Know About Myanmar’s Chaotic War
summary: The Southeast Asian nation recently experienced its highest monthly civilian death toll since the war began in 2021.

title: Supreme Court Bars Rastafarian Man From Suing Prison Guards for Shaving His Dreadlocks
summary: Damon Landor, a Rastafarian, tried to sue Louisiana prison officials for violating his religious rights.

title: Supreme Court Rejects Lawsuit Claiming Cisco Systems Helped China Target Falun Gong
summary: The court’s decision could have broader implications for whether companies can be held liable for aiding in international human rights abuses.

title: The Heat Wave in Europe Is Hitting Records. Here’s What to Know.
summary: France had its hottest June day ever, with forecasters in Britain predicting that the record for the highest temperature for this time of year was also likely to be broken.

title: How Trump Turned America’s Refugee Program Into a Pathway for White People
summary: President Trump has created an exception to his refugee ban for white South Africans, reshaping a program intended for people fleeing persecution and disaster.

title: A Look Inside the Welcome Bags Planned for White South African Refugees
summary: The United States is putting together bags with a children’s book on so-called reverse racism, and with a document that defends the country’s founding on the basis of slavery.

title: Federal Judge Bars ICE From Making Arrests in Immigration Courts
summary: The ruling in a class-action lawsuit filed in California applies to immigration courts nationwide.

title: Meta Has Created a Prediction Markets App
summary: The experimental app, internally called “Arena,” would be independent of Facebook and Instagram. It could compete for attention with Polymarket and Kalshi, the biggest prediction markets.

title: U.S. Presses Meta to Agree to A.I. Reviews
summary: Federal officials are urging the lone major tech company holdout to allow government safety evaluations, weeks after ordering Anthropic to pull its latest model.

title: Looking Back on the U.S.A.’s Many Founders, 250 Years In
summary: In the past 50 years, the way we tell the story of the Revolution has become dramatically more complex. Can it still inspire us all?

title: U.S. Support Is Israel’s True Weakness
summary: Israel’s dependence on the United States has allowed it to ignore the need for diplomacy.

title: If You Love America, Cringe for It
summary: Let’s remember who we once were, because it’s what we may yet be again.

title: Josh Johnson Muses on the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool
summary: “Hey, kids, remember you wanted to go to Disneyland? Instead, we’re going to go see the world’s largest kombucha!” Johnson said of the pool renovation as a tourist stop.

title: Asia Tech Shares Swing Wildly as A.I. Jitters Persist
summary: The turbulence in Asia’s chip-dominated stock markets highlighted how heavily global equities have come to depend on enthusiasm for artificial intelligence.
